article.title: Small Business Homepage Optimization: The First 10 Seconds

blog.title: Small Business Homepage Optimization: The First 10 Seconds

facebook.caption:
Small Business Homepage Optimization: The First 10 Seconds

Most small-business homepages lose buyers before the first scroll: unclear offer, buried proof, soft CTA, or too much friction. This refresh breaks down the first 10 seconds that decide whether a visitor becomes a lead.

Read it: https://eb28.co/blog/small-business-homepage-optimization-the-first-10-seconds/

linkedin.caption:
New EB28 growth brief: Small Business Homepage Optimization: The First 10 Seconds

The practical homepage test: can a buyer understand what you do, why to trust you, and what to do next in the first 10 seconds? The guide ties above-the-fold copy, proof, forms, internal links, and fast follow-up into one lead path.

https://eb28.co/blog/small-business-homepage-optimization-the-first-10-seconds/

instagram.caption:
small business homepage optimization: the first 10 seconds.

Clear offer. Visible proof. Short path to action. Fast follow-up.

Read the full guide at eb28.co/blog/
instagram.creativeBrief: Carousel: Slide 1 first-10-seconds test, Slide 2 clarify the offer, Slide 3 move proof near the CTA, Slide 4 reduce form friction, Slide 5 route follow-up fast, Slide 6 CTA to the Melbourne Web Studio quiz.

shortFormVideo.hook: Your homepage has about 10 seconds to prove it is worth trusting.
shortFormVideo.brief: 30-45 second vertical video for owners: show a generic homepage hero, call out unclear offer/proof/CTA/form friction, then show the EB28 checklist path and close on the Melbourne Web Studio quiz.
script: Most small-business homepages do not fail because they are ugly. They fail because a buyer cannot tell what to do next.
In the first 10 seconds, your page needs a plain offer, visible proof, and one obvious CTA.
Then the form and follow-up have to match the promise, because delayed response kills warm leads.
EB28 refreshed the homepage optimization checklist so local businesses can fix the leak without starting from a full rebuild.
